name: Meeting Brief - Backstory Starter
description: Prepares an AI-generated briefing document before each upcoming meeting. A parent cron workflow fires every 15 minutes and invokes this sub-workflow for meetings approaching on the calendar. The workflow fetches account context from Backstory via MCP — recent activity, engagement history, key contacts — and passes it to the LLM to produce a concise meeting brief. The brief is delivered to the meeting owner via Messaging (Slack, Teams, or Email) so they walk in fully prepared.

nodes.notes: Sub-workflow (called by Meeting Prep Cron, every 15 min) |

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: List meetings starting in the next 30 minutes and keep only external customer-facing calls. Replace the placeholder API endpoints and tokens with real systems before enabling this workflow.

nodes.name: Analyze Meeting Brief

parameters.text:
=You are generating the Meeting Brief workflow output.

PURPOSE:
Prepares an AI-generated briefing document before each upcoming meeting. A parent cron workflow fires every 15 minutes and invokes this sub-workflow for meetings approaching on the calendar. The workflow fetches account context from Backstory via MCP — recent activity, engagement history, key contacts — and passes it to the LLM to produce a concise meeting brief. The brief is delivered to the meeting owner via Messaging (Slack, Teams, or Email) so they walk in fully prepared.

SOURCE PAYLOAD:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.record, null, 2) }}

TASKS:
1. Use Backstory MCP to enrich the record with the most relevant account, opportunity, engagement, or stakeholder context.
2. Decide what matters most for the meeting brief use case.
3. Produce a concise markdown summary with: headline, key findings, risks, recommended actions, and routing notes if applicable.

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
- Keep it compact enough for Slack.
- Prefer bullets.
- Mention concrete dates, owners, amounts, or stages when the data provides them.
- If the source record is incomplete, explicitly say what is missing.

REFERENCE STYLE:
📋 **Meeting Brief** — ACME Corp Technical Review | Today 2:00 PM

👥 **ATTENDEES:**
- @sarah.chen (Account Owner) + @james.park (SE)
- Dan Reeves (VP Engineering, ACME) — Decision maker, attended 3 of last 4 calls
- Lisa Wong (Director of IT, ACME) — Technical champion, drove POC approval
- New: Kevin Marsh (Security Architect) — First time joining, likely for compliance review

📊 **ACCOUNT CONTEXT:**
- Deal: ===$425,000=== | Stage: Technical Validation | Close: 04/2026

options.systemMessage: You are a Backstory workflow analyst generating Meeting Brief outputs. Keep the response actionable, concrete, and Slack-friendly.

nodes.notes: Wire your preferred LLM credentials to the chat model node after import.

nodes.name: Backstory MCP
nodes.notes: This mirrors the existing workflow infrastructure in the repo. Add MCP auth headers after import.

nodes.name: Aggregate Results
nodes.notes: Collapses all analyzed records into a single outbound summary. Replace this with per-owner routing if you want one message per rep/CSM/executive.

nodes.name: Post to Slack
nodes.notes: Swap this node for Teams or SMTP if Slack is not your delivery target.
